verdict: Moderate displacement risk. Veterinarian scores 80 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

timeline.description: AI-powered diagnostic tools become widely available for veterinary use
Veterinary schools begin incorporating AI and machine learning into curricula

key_tasks: Treat sick or injured animals by prescribing medication, setting bones, dressing wounds, or performing surgery.
Examine animals to detect and determine the nature of diseases or injuries.
Operate diagnostic equipment, such as radiographic or ultrasound equipment, and interpret the resulting images.

comparisons.name: Dentist
comparisons.description: Similar tasks in dentistry with a stronger emphasis on physical dexterity

comparisons.name: Surgeon
comparisons.description: Similar tasks in surgery with a stronger emphasis on physical dexterity and regulatory moat

evidence_ledger.task: Treat sick or injured animals by prescribing medication, setting bones, dressing wounds, or performing surgery.
Examine animals to detect and determine the nature of diseases or injuries.
Operate diagnostic equipment, such as radiographic or ultrasound equipment, and interpret the resulting images.

reasoning_steps.description: Veterinarians perform tasks that are highly routine and pattern-matching (e.g., treating sick animals), but also require creative judgment and physical dexterity.
reasoning_steps.impact: AI can automate some tasks, but not all

reasoning_steps.description: The regulatory moat for veterinarians is relatively weak compared to other professions (e.g., dentistry, surgery).

adjacent_careers.name: Dentist
adjacent_careers.description: Similar tasks in dentistry with a stronger emphasis on physical dexterity

adjacent_careers.name: Surgeon
adjacent_careers.description: Similar tasks in surgery with a stronger emphasis on physical dexterity and regulatory moat

action_plan.description: Pursue additional education or training in AI and machine learning for veterinary applications.
action_plan.impact: Develop skills to work with AI-assisted diagnosis tools

action_plan.description: Explore opportunities to work with AI-powered diagnostic tools and develop expertise in their application.
action_plan.impact: Stay competitive in the job market

follow_up_suggestions.name: Research AI-powered diagnostic tools for veterinary applications
follow_up_suggestions.description: Understand the current state of AI technology in veterinary medicine

follow_up_suggestions.name: Network with professionals in the field to learn about their experiences with AI and machine learning.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Gain insights into the impact of AI on the profession

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

recent_developments.headline: Hospitals deploy ambient AI scribes at scale
recent_developments.impact: Major US health systems report clinicians saving 1–2 hours of documentation per day.

recent_developments.headline: Drug discovery accelerates with foundation models
recent_developments.impact: AI-designed candidates are entering Phase I trials within months instead of years.
